original_query: select dname, ddeptno, c from             (select name dname, deptno ddeptno, count(*) as c             from dept group by grouping sets ((name,deptno),(name))) t              where dname = 'Charlie';
rewritten_query: select dname, ddeptno, c from             (select name dname, deptno ddeptno, count(*) as c             from dept group by grouping sets ((name,deptno),(name))) t              where dname = 'Charlie';

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Move the filter condition 'dname = 'Charlie'' from the outer query into the subquery as 'name = 'Charlie'' to filter rows before grouping and aggregation
Remove the redundant outer WHERE clause since the filtering is now done in the subquery
